rules.title: SQL Injection
rules.description: Raw string interpolation in SQL queries. Use parameterized queries instead.

rules.title: Command Injection
rules.description: Shell command built with string interpolation. Use execFile/spawn with arguments array instead.

rules.title: Hardcoded Secret
rules.description: Hardcoded credentials should be replaced with environment variables or a secret manager.

rules.title: Dangerous eval Usage
rules.description: eval() executes arbitrary code. Use safer alternatives like JSON.parse or Function constructor (with caution).

rules.title: XSS via innerHTML / outerHTML
rules.description: Direct innerHTML/outerHTML assignment can lead to XSS. Use textContent or createElement instead.

rules.title: Insecure Deserialization
rules.description: Untrusted data passed to deserialization can lead to RCE. Use safe parsing (e.g., validate input, use allowlist).

rules.title: Path Traversal
rules.description: User-controlled path concatenation can enable path traversal attacks. Use path.resolve with allowlist.

rules.title: User Input Reflected in Response
rules.description: Reflecting unsanitized user input in HTTP responses can lead to XSS. Use proper encoding/sanitization.

rules.title: Prototype Pollution via Merge/Assign
rules.description: Unsafe object merge with user input can pollute Object.prototype. Use defensive merge or Object.hasOwn checks.

rules.title: Weak or Custom Cryptography
rules.description: Weak hash algorithms (MD5, SHA1) or custom crypto primitives should not be used in production.

rules.title: Permissive CORS
rules.description: CORS configured to allow all origins can expose your API to cross-origin attacks.

rules.title: Insecure HTTP Connection
rules.description: Using http:// instead of https:// in production exposes data in transit.

rules.title: Debug Mode Enabled
rules.description: Debug/verbose logging in production can leak sensitive information.

rules.title: Potentially Unsafe Regular Expression
rules.description: Regular expression with nested quantifiers outside character classes can cause ReDoS.
